citations.desc: Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries.
citations.narrative:
The inspector arrived at the facility during their optimal hours for inspection. The licensee was present but stated
numerous times that she would not allow an inspection to be conducted. Failure to allow an inspection interferes with the
ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals are being provided with adequate veterinary care and being
maintained in compliant conditions.
Each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler, or carrier, shall, during regular business hours, allow APHIS officials to enter
its place of business, examine required records and make copies of such records, and to inspect and photograph the
facilities, property and animals, as the APHIS officials consider necessary to enforce the provisions of the Act and
regulations, and to document by photography and other means, conditions of noncompliance. The licensee must allow
APHIS officials to conduct inspections of the animals, property, and records during regular business hours to ensure
compliance with the Animal Welfare Act and its regulations.
Correction date: Beginning March 26, 2026, the licensee must allow APHIS officials to conduct inspections.
An exit interview could not be conducted.
n

narrative:
2.126(a)  Critical
   
Access and inspection of records and property; submission of itineraries. 
The inspector arrived at the facility during their optimal hours for inspection. The licensee was present but stated 
numerous times that she would not allow an inspection to be conducted. Failure to allow an inspection interferes with the 
ability of APHIS officials to determine if the animals are being provided with adequate veterinary care and being 
maintained in compliant conditions. 
 
Each dealer, exhibitor, intermediate handler, or carrier, shall, during regular business hours, allow APHIS officials to enter 
its place of business, examine required records and make copies of such records, and to inspect and photograph the 
facilities, property and animals, as the APHIS officials consider necessary to enforce the provisions of the Act and 
regulations, and to document by photography and other means, conditions of noncompliance. The licensee must allow 
APHIS officials to conduct inspections of the animals, property, and records during regular business hours to ensure 
compliance with the Animal Welfare Act and its regulations.  
 
Correction date: Beginning March 26, 2026, the licensee must allow APHIS officials to conduct inspections. 
 
An exit interview could not be conducted.
